acceptance_targets: [chapter_14_F01] PASS if the revised section from snapshots/cycle_01/chapters/chapter_14.md:161 through snapshots/cycle_01/chapters/chapter_14.md:317 dramatizes no more than four clause battles and each retained clause produces a distinct leverage shift (new concession, new threat, or new revealed motive). FAIL if three consecutive clauses still follow near-identical proposal/objection/compromise beats.

[chapter_14_F02] PASS if the revised passage spanning snapshots/cycle_01/chapters/chapter_14.md:153 to snapshots/cycle_01/chapters/chapter_14.md:221 contains at least three explicit Anika interior beats and no gap longer than six paragraphs without interiority. FAIL if a 10+ paragraph external-only stretch remains.

[FBR-05] PASS if the revised spans remain intelligible when dialogue tags are removed and each speaker is still identifiable by register/rhythm alone; FAIL if lines remain easily interchangeable across the three speakers.

findings.acceptance_test: PASS if the revised section from snapshots/cycle_01/chapters/chapter_14.md:161 through snapshots/cycle_01/chapters/chapter_14.md:317 dramatizes no more than four clause battles and each retained clause produces a distinct leverage shift (new concession, new threat, or new revealed motive). FAIL if three consecutive clauses still follow near-identical proposal/objection/compromise beats.
findings.problem: The clause-by-clause negotiation repeats the same objection/override rhythm across too many turns, so leverage shifts flatten and momentum stalls before the signature phase.
findings.rewrite_direction: Restructure the negotiation around the highest-stakes pivots at snapshots/cycle_01/chapters/chapter_14.md:161, snapshots/cycle_01/chapters/chapter_14.md:173, snapshots/cycle_01/chapters/chapter_14.md:231, and snapshots/cycle_01/chapters/chapter_14.md:275; collapse lower-conflict logistics from snapshots/cycle_01/chapters/chapter_14.md:283 and snapshots/cycle_01/chapters/chapter_14.md:291 into brief summary language near snapshots/cycle_01/chapters/chapter_14.md:297 so the scene reaches signatures with sharper escalation.

findings.acceptance_test: PASS if the revised passage spanning snapshots/cycle_01/chapters/chapter_14.md:153 to snapshots/cycle_01/chapters/chapter_14.md:221 contains at least three explicit Anika interior beats and no gap longer than six paragraphs without interiority. FAIL if a 10+ paragraph external-only stretch remains.
findings.problem: There is a long screenplay-like run where focalized interiority drops out; the chapter spends over ten consecutive paragraphs in external negotiation before returning to Anika's inner experience.
findings.rewrite_direction: Add concise Anika interior beats at key turns around snapshots/cycle_01/chapters/chapter_14.md:169, snapshots/cycle_01/chapters/chapter_14.md:187, snapshots/cycle_01/chapters/chapter_14.md:205, and snapshots/cycle_01/chapters/chapter_14.md:215 (sensation, memory, or self-assessment) so her decision pressure is continuously legible rather than only reappearing at snapshots/cycle_01/chapters/chapter_14.md:221.

findings.acceptance_test: PASS if the revised spans remain intelligible when dialogue tags are removed and each speaker is still identifiable by register/rhythm alone; FAIL if lines remain easily interchangeable across the three speakers.
findings.problem: Dialogue register convergence in conflict scenes: Anika, Ravi, and Priya frequently share the same polished legal-therapeutic cadence, reducing idiolect separation and age-specific speech texture required by the style bible.
findings.rewrite_direction: Revoice mediation exchanges at snapshots/cycle_01/FINAL_NOVEL.md:4970-4993, snapshots/cycle_01/FINAL_NOVEL.md:5058-5073, snapshots/cycle_01/FINAL_NOVEL.md:5080-5097, and snapshots/cycle_01/FINAL_NOVEL.md:5134-5153 so each speaker carries distinct syntax and lexicon: Anika (short, concrete, pressure-fragmented), Ravi (work/process terms with variable contraction under stress), Priya (plain boundary language without courtroom theatrics).

non_negotiables: Maintain heading contract exactly (# Chapter N).
Elliptical clipping is not permitted in dialogue or narration.
Avoid transcript cadence in high-stakes dialogue.
Avoid stiff over-formality in dialogue; use contractions and colloquial rhythm where character-true.
Reduce procedural drift when it displaces embodied consequence.
Maintain character-specific voice signatures from style bible.
Honor aesthetic risk policy: sanitization is the primary craft risk — do not soften, euphemize, or retreat from the story's established register. When in doubt, err toward rawness.

revision_pass.focus: Prioritize structural and craft blockers first: causality, chapter architecture, stakes clarity, continuity logic, and all HIGH/CRITICAL defects.
revision_pass.label: HIGH/CRITICAL structural/craft fixes

aesthetic_risk_policy.creative_risk_policy: Lean into uncomfortable specificity when it changes behavior: pain detail, debt arithmetic, manipulative care, and mixed motives. In edge cases, prefer the truest in-character action over protective narration, then carry the real consequence forward instead of immediately moralizing it away.
aesthetic_risk_policy.euphemism_penalty: If coercion, grief, injury, or economic shame appears, language must stay specific and embodied; vague softening triggers mandatory rewrite.

dialogue_rules.default_contraction_use: High - contractions are the spoken default; uncontracted forms are reserved for formal authority, deliberate emphasis, or emotional freezing.

prose_style_profile.chapter_texture_variance: Vary scene count and dialogue density chapter to chapter: rotate match compression, slow inventory aftermath, institutional pressure scenes, and quiet domestic intervals so adjacent chapters do not share the same structural pulse.
prose_style_profile.diction: Concrete contemporary diction with class-aware specificity; avoid abstract therapy language unless a character would actually use it.
prose_style_profile.narrative_distance: Close third anchored in Anika for most chapters, with brief controlled slips toward Ravi in designated confrontation and aftermath chapters.
prose_style_profile.rhythm_target: Alternate clipped athletic sequences with longer reflective cadences; sentence length should track exertion, shame, and release.
